function_explanation: Computes the centered 2-dimensional Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) of an input array by applying an inverse shift before the transform and a forward shift afterwards.
function_docstring:
Centered 2D FFT.

    This function performs a centered 2D Fast Fourier Transform. It first shifts the
    zero-frequency component to the corner of the spectrum (using `ifftshift`), computes
    the 2D FFT, and then shifts the zero-frequency component back to the center of the
    spectrum (using `fftshift`). This is commonly used in image processing and optics
    to keep the DC component in the center of the array.

    Args:
        x (numpy.ndarray): Input array of shape (..., M, N). The FFT is computed over
            the last two axes.

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: The centered 2D FFT of the input, with the same shape as `x`.
            The zero-frequency component is located at the center of the last two dimensions.
usage_example:
import numpy as np
import scipy.fft
from agent_fft2c import fft2c

# Create a 2D Gaussian signal
N = 128
x = np.linspace(-2, 2, N)
y = np.linspace(-2, 2, N)
X, Y = np.meshgrid(x, y)
signal = np.exp(-(X**2 + Y**2))

# Compute the centered 2D FFT
fft_result = fft2c(signal)

# The zero-frequency component is at the center
center_idx = N // 2
print(f"DC component magnitude: {np.abs(fft_result[center_idx, center_idx]):.2f}")
